top_issues.issue: Large File / God Module: 7987 lines combining event loop, input state machine, all command dispatch, substitute/global/search execution, gutter rendering, and test suite. Exceeds any reasonable single-responsibility boundary.

Large Class / God Object: EditorState has 39 public fields covering buffer, cursor, viewport, keymaps, registers, undo/redo, macros, marks, jump list, change list, search, theme, panels, and focus. Single mutable container aggregates all editor subsystems.
Long Method: register_builtin_commands is ~887 lines, registering ~30+ command handlers inline using repeated crate:: path prefixes rather than a data-driven table.
Long Method: handle_key_event is ~605 lines, handling 12+ distinct InputState variants, count accumulation, self-insert, and all motion/operator dispatch in a single function body.

Feature Envy / Insider Trading: 5198-line bridge module directly accesses 8+ alfred_core sub-modules (buffer, cursor, viewport, hook, command, theme, panel, editor_state) via repeated Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> borrows, creating intense structural coupling.
Message Chain / Insider Trading: 355 occurrences of alfred_core:: fully-qualified path in app.rs, indicating the TUI layer reaches deeply into core internals rather than going through a facade.
Conditional Complexity: execute_colon_command uses multi-arm string matching (q, q!, wq, w, w <path>, e <path>, eval, g/, g!/, v/, s/, %s/) all as one match; brittle against extension.

Divergent Change: app.rs must be modified when adding any of: new InputState variant, new colon command, new deferred action type, new Vim motion, or new rendering hook. Multiple reasons to change in one file.
Shotgun Surgery: adding a new editor subsystem (e.g., tabs or split panes) requires adding field(s) to EditorState, updating new(), and updating any snapshot/undo types — scattered across the file.

Tramp Data: dummy EditorState created and discarded when calling Dynamic (Lisp) command handlers so the closure can use its own captured Rc<RefCell<EditorState>> — a workaround for the borrow-checker.
Duplicated Code: execute_delete_with_motion and execute_yank_with_motion share the same CharWise/LineWise pattern but with different operations; the range-computation logic is repeated.
Dead Variable: PluginRegistry built during load_plugins() is immediately dropped (let _ = reg) rather than stored on EditorState. Noted as future work but creates confusion now.
Dead Assignment: let _lisp_env = runtime.env() in register_define_command is assigned but immediately annotated as no longer used. Should be removed.
Boolean Blindness: execute_global_delete uses a plain bool invert parameter. A small enum (MatchingLines / NonMatchingLines) would be clearer.
